channel.title: maGaming RSS Feed - www.mmorpg.com
channel.description: A cleaned-up version of the original gaming feed for www.mmorpg.com

item.title: Diablo Immortal Adds New Features, LEgendary Affixes, and Matchmaking Improvements
item.content:encoded:
Diablo Immortal is getting a significant update this week. There are new Legendary Affixes and more, but taking center stage are new features and improvements, including Auto-Battle, open captain selection, and improvements to strengthen competition in Cycle of Strife.

The update will be available globally by Wednesday. When the update goes live, there are new Legendary Affixes that will drop in Inferno XII and up. The selection is wide-ranging, with new goodies to seek out to further customize your build.

Auto-Battle makes its debut in this patch, unlocking at level 60. This is a feature primarily aimed at more casual players and those with more limited time in which to make progress. If you enable the feature, your character will be automatically controlled at your current location and fight enemies in the nearby area. You can only use Auto Battle for 1 hour at a time and only 24 hours per week. and there are limitations.

This is not going to be as efficient as actively playing the game, but it will be helpful. If you tune your build, and adjust things like auto health potion use and Life threshold for use, you can get a little more out of Auto Battle. But this is always intended to be a supplement rather than the main way you progress and the limits should keep it from getting out of hand.

The other big change in the patch is open captain selection in Battleground mode. Designed to help team balance and offer choice over composition in matchmaking, when all players matched are Legendary rank or above, a Legendary Duel may trigger. What this means is that the two highest rated players become team captains and each captain will select three teammates in a draft format with 15 seconds to make a pick in a structured order.  any other players will be assigned by the system.

Cross-region Battlegrounds matchmaking also expands in SEA, Japan, Korea, and Oceania regions.

To improve competition in the Cycle of Strife, the team is adding a hidden matching system within Rite of Exile. Leaders can discover the hidden matches via scouting points earned from weekly duties. Scouting is guaranteed to find the Shadow clans in the match orders and participation from Immortals. The number of victories required in week 7 for Rite of Exile will come down from four to three, which should increase the field of teams competing.

Challenge Rift Season 18 opens on May 4th  through June 17th, with 50 new floors to take on. Floors 731-750 will be lowered to make those more doable. There's also a new Battle Pass coming on May 7th, a refresh of the Legendary Gem drop pool on 4/29, and damage reduction increases in PvP.

You can check all the details for these and more changes in the patch notes.



https://www.mmorpg.com/news/diablo-immortal-adds-new-features-legendary-affixes-and-matchmaking-improvements-2000137907
item.dc:creator: invalid@example.com (ResetEra_Newsbot

item.title: RuneScape Reduces Visual Clutter in Combat, Adds Better Targeting, and Double XP Returns This Week, With Tweaks
item.content:encoded:
The latest stop on RuneScape’s Road to Restoration has to do with reducing visual clutter, particularly during combat. Double XP is also returning this week, and there’s a new free gift bundle to claim.

With the Road to Restoration, Jagex aims to improve Gielinor and the overall RuneScape experience and they've been moving at a rapid pace to make these improvements since they were announced just a couple of months ago. The latest part of the ongoing project is reworking some combat related visuals to be less cluttered and to be more intuitive and helpful.

The team, in recent years, moved towards using squares, which are in line with the tile-based movement. Now, they are adding walk markers and target reticules. In the update notes (and in the header image here), you can see this in action with the target reticule around the boss. Its size is telling, and this will help you target and decide how to strategize and what you should be using. These will scale with target size. 

Walk markers are also available for additional clarity  and guidance.

If you don't want one or both of them, you can toggle them on or off in the Settings menu. 

The team also modifies a number of visual effects to make them less obtrusive and there are a list of examples in the patch notes so you can get an idea of the before and after. 

Double XP is returning on May 1st, with several changes based on prior Road to Restoration updates. What hasn't changed is you still get 48 hours of Double XP to use when you want over the 10-day period. What has changed is no more daily login incentives (after DailyScape changes). This will also be the final time that you can earn Double XP rokens and use them at the store. 

If you have any tokens you should spend them at the Double XP Token Store,  because this event will be the last time the shop is available and any leftover tokens you have after the event ends will be converted into GP. More details on this event are in the patch notes, including outfit availability, and more that will shift based on their “integrity” efforts.

There is a free gift bundle in the Marketplace to claim, now that Solomon has closed shop: Solomon's Loyalty Point Bundle.



https://www.mmorpg.com/news/runescape-reduces-visual-clutter-in-combat-adds-better-targeting-and-double-xp-returns-this-week-with-tweaks-2000137908
item.dc:creator: invalid@example.com (ResetEra_Newsbot
